title: Sales Training & Enablement Manager
description:
**Summary**
-----------


The Sales Training \& Enablement Manager is a strategic individual contributor responsible for designing, delivering, and continuously improving enablement programs that enhance sales effectiveness and drive revenue performance. This role partners cross\-functionally to translate business strategy and product capabilities into practical, field\-ready training, tools, and playbooks that accelerate seller productivity. The position requires the ability to independently manage priorities, drive initiatives, and deliver measurable impact on seller performance and commercial outcomes. This position can be based in Columbus, OH (hybrid) or a remote position if based in the US within the Eastern time zone with frequent travel to the Ohio location.

**Essential Duties and Responsibilities**
-----------------------------------------

* Build a strong foundation through structured onboarding that develops core commercial competencies—territory management, pipeline discipline, value articulation, and customer engagement—while establishing product fluency and a clear understanding of our right to win.
* Create role\-specific learning paths and certification programs that accelerate ramp time and guide sellers from basic proficiency to advanced skills in problem framing, discovery, objection handling, opportunity strategy, and influencing customer decision\-making.
* Design engaging, continuous skill\-development experiences (workshops, role\-plays, scenario\-based training, self\-guided learning) that strengthen a seller’s ability to articulate value, position solutions to customer problems, and drive both net new acquisition and net revenue retention.
* Standardize sales execution by codifying messaging, qualification standards, talk tracks, territory strategies, and sales plays that help sellers prioritize accounts, engage productively, win opportunities faster, and increase deal velocity.
* Use data, call insights, and field feedback to continually refine enablement programs, improve seller behaviors and commercial effectiveness, and drive measurable improvements in win rates, pipeline health, and customer engagement quality.
* Partner cross\-functionally across Product, Product Marketing, BD, and RevOps to translate complex capabilities into clear field\-ready narratives—playbooks, competitive positioning, pitch frameworks, and launch enablement—to ensure the field is consistently prepared for GTM shifts and strategic initiatives.
**Qualifications**
------------------


To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

**Education and/or Work Experience**
------------------------------------

* 7\+ years of experience in sales, sales enablement, sales operations, or commercial strategy roles
* Proven direct sales experience with demonstrated success in meeting or exceeding revenue targets
* Proven experience designing and delivering sales training, onboarding, or enablement programs that drive measurable performance outcomes
* Experience working in a remote or highly distributed environment with demonstrated ability to operate independently and manage priorities effectively
**Technical Skills/Physical Requirements**
------------------------------------------

* Strong understanding of sales processes, pipeline management, and customer engagement best practices
* Highly self\-motivated and organized, with the ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines with minimal supervision
* Strong analytical mindset with experience using data, insights, and feedback to refine programs and improve outcomes
* Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate complex concepts into clear, actionable guidance for sales teams
* Demonstrated ability to influence without authority and drive adoption across cross\-functional stakeholders

title: Sales Enablement Specialist
description:
Saviynt's AI\-powered identity platform manages and governs human and non\-human access to all of an organization's applications, data, and business processes. Customers trust Saviynt to safeguard their digital assets, drive operational efficiency, and reduce compliance costs. Built for the AI age, Saviynt is today helping organizations safely accelerate their deployment and usage of AI. Saviynt is recognized as the leader in identity security, with solutions that protect and empower the world’s leading brands, Fortune 500 companies and government institutions. For more information, please visit www.saviynt.com.  

Saviynt is seeking a Sales Enablement Specialist to provide tactical execution and operational support for our sales enablement programs. Reporting to the Director of Sales Enablement, this role is responsible for managing sales content, training programs, enablement tools, and operational processes that enhance seller productivity.  

  

 This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, detail\-oriented, and passionate about enabling sales teams by ensuring they have the right resources, tools, and support to succeed. The Sales Enablement Specialist will work closely with Sales, Marketing, Product, and Revenue Operations to support the execution of high\-impact enablement initiatives.
  

  

The Sales Enablement Team at Saviynt empowers our internal sales ecosystem with strategic enablement programs that foster knowledge, skills, and engagement to drive revenue growth. We develop comprehensive enablement frameworks that equip sellers with the tools, training, and insights needed to effectively communicate Saviynt’s value, accelerate deal cycles, and improve customer outcomes.  



### **WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING**

**Content Management \& Sales Tool Optimization**
* Maintain and organize sales content in enablement platforms like Highspot, ensuring materials are up to date and easily accessible.
* Assist in the curation and governance of sales playbooks, battlecards, competitive insights, and other training materials.
* Support content governance and lifecycle management, ensuring that outdated or irrelevant resources are archived or updated.
* Drive best practices for content utilization, optimizing searchability and relevance for sellers.

**Training \& Sales Onboarding Support**
* Assist in the execution of new hire onboarding programs, ensuring seamless access to training materials and certifications.
* Coordinate and support ongoing training sessions, sales bootcamps, and role\-based enablement programs.

Track training completion rates and maintain dashboards to ensure high engagement and continuous learning.  
* 

**Operational \& Administrative Enablement Support**
* Serve as a first point of contact for sales reps needing assistance with enablement tools, training resources, or content access.
* Manage scheduling and logistics for enablement programs, webinars, and sales training sessions.
* Provide technical and administrative support for sales enablement tools, assisting with system updates, troubleshooting, and reporting.
* Maintain and update sales enablement resources, ensuring sellers have one\-stop access to enablement resources.

**Data Analysis \& Reporting**
* Track and report on enablement metrics, including training completion rates, content engagement, and tool adoption.
* Provide insights on content effectiveness and training impact, helping refine enablement programs over time.
* Assist in surveying sales teams to gather feedback on enablement programs, ensuring ongoing improvements.
### **WHAT YOU BRING**

* 5 years of experience in sales enablement, sales operations, marketing, and/or training \& development.
* Experience working with sales enablement tools (e.g., Highspot, Showpad, or similar) and CRM systems like Salesforce.
* Strong organizational and project management skills, with the ability to prioritize multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to create clear, structured training and enablement content.
* Analytical mindset, with experience tracking enablement KPIs, reporting on program effectiveness, and driving data\-driven improvements.
* Self\-starter mentality, capable of working independently while effectively collaborating with cross\-functional teams.
* Experience working in B2B SaaS, cybersecurity, or technology sales environments is a plus.
* Familiarity with Command of the Message and/or MEDDPICC methodologies is a plus.
**If required for this role, you will:*** Complete security \& privacy literacy and awareness training during onboarding and annually thereafter
* Review (initially and annually thereafter), understand, and adhere to Information Security/Privacy Policies and Procedures such as (but not limited to):

  

\> Data Classification, Retention \& Handling Policy
\> Incident Response Policy/Procedures
\> Business Continuity/Disaster Recovery Policy/Procedures
\> Mobile Device Policy
\> Account Management Policy
\> Access Control Policy
\> Personnel Security Policy
\> Privacy Policy  

Saviynt is an amazing place to work. We are a high\-growth, Platform as a Service company focused on Identity Authority to power and protect the world at work. You will experience tremendous growth and learning opportunities through challenging yet rewarding work which directly impacts our customers, all within a welcoming and positive work environment. If you're resilient and enjoy working in a dynamic environment you belong with us! ***Saviynt is an equal opportunity employer and we welcome everyone to our team. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or veteran status.***
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Market Enablement Lead
description:
Content
Permanent
New York, London or Stockholm

Deliver creativity to the world — one note, one voice, one idea at a time. At Spotify, we focus relentlessly on building the best and most valuable experience available anywhere, enhancing every moment by connecting the world to the art and the creatives that shape it.



We're growing our world\-class Audiobooks Editorial team and are looking for a Market Enablement Manager to evolve and lead the standards, frameworks, and systems that power Audiobooks editorial at global scale. As Spotify expands Audiobooks into new markets and regions, this role works in close partnership with our regional editorial leads and cross\-functional stakeholders to bring Audiobooks to life locally. You'll be the custodian of editorial quality, making sure our human editorial judgement is brought consistently to new and distributed markets, from the same foundation of taste, standards, and craft.



What You'll Do


+ Own editorial launch readiness for markets where dedicated regional editorial coverage has not yet been established, including primary responsibility for upcoming shared World English curation, ensuring high\-quality, culturally resonant content is ready for listeners from day one.
+ Design and maintain the editorial frameworks, quality standards, and programming guidelines that enable consistent, high\-quality Audiobooks curation across Spotify's growing market footprint
+ Build and scale the tools, playbooks, and onboarding resources that allow regional editors, market partners, and cross\-functional teams to programme Audiobooks confidently and independently
+ Collaborate closely with regional editorial leads to ensure Market Enablement frameworks are grounded in on\-the\-ground editorial reality and serve the needs of editors working directly in market
+ Identify editorial coverage needs across Spotify's markets and develop scalable solutions — from shared curation models to AI\-assisted workflows — to meet them
+ Lead the editorial systems and tooling roadmap in collaboration with Product and Engineering, including catalog tagging, editorial scoring, and workflow infrastructure
+ Build and manage the Market Enablement function's editorial calendar, ensuring alignment with global launch priorities, cultural moments, and cross\-functional initiatives
+ Define and monitor quality signals across distributed markets, surfacing insights to regional editors and leadership to drive continuous improvement
+ Partner with the Director of Editorial to develop the long\-term global standards as Audiobooks scales
Who You Are


+ You have 7\+ years of experience in editorial, content strategy, or programming — ideally at a global digital platform, streaming service, or major publisher
+ You have a demonstrated track record of building editorial systems, frameworks, or standards that scale across teams, markets, or content types
+ You understand how editorial quality works in practice — you've made programming decisions yourself and can translate that judgment into structures others can use
+ You are an exceptional communicator who can make complex editorial concepts accessible to non\-editorial partners across functions and markets
+ You have experience working with or alongside market, regional, or international teams and understand the tension between global consistency and local relevance
+ You are curious about AI tools and editorial technology, and have thought seriously about how they can augment — not replace — human editorial judgment at scale
+ You are a natural systems thinker who sees patterns, builds processes, and creates clarity in ambiguous, fast\-moving environments
+ You care deeply about books and are passionate about the evolution of audio and spoken\-word storytelling
+ You communicate clearly, prioritize ruthlessly, and thrive in collaborative, cross\-functional environments
Where You'll Be



This role is based in New York, London or Stockholm


+ We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home
+ We ask that you come in 2\-3 times per week.
Learn about life at Spotify


The United States base range for this position is $122,507 to $175,010, plus equity. The benefits available for this position include health insurance, six month paid parental leave, 401(k) retirement plan, monthly meal allowance, 23 paid days off, 13 paid flexible holidays, paid sick leave. These ranges may be modified in the future.  

Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward\-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.  

At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know \- we’re here to support you in any way we can.  

Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008\. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.
Our global benefits


Extensive learning opportunities, through our dedicated team, GreenHouse.
Flexible share incentives letting you choose how you share in our success.
Global parental leave, six months off \- for all new parents.
All The Feels, our employee assistance program and self\-care hub.
Flexible public holidays, swap days off according to your values and beliefs.

title: Senior GTM Enablement Manager
description:
**About Backstory**



Backstory (formerly People.ai) is the leading AI answers platform for sales teams. With more than a decade of training AI models on billions of sales interactions, Backstory enables modern sales teams to ask questions and get the right answer in the moment directly within their existing AI workflows. Sales leaders from companies like Red Hat, Five9, Palo Alto Networks, Iron Mountain, TransUnion, Randstad and others rely on Backstory to get trusted answers wrapped in real business context helping them orchestrate deals more effectively and drive revenue.



By connecting every signal to the right accounts and opportunities, Backstory shows teams exactly what is happening and what to do next. Customers across industries including enterprise, cybersecurity, data and analytics, and defense use Backstory to drive trillions in pipeline and revenue.



Recognized by Gartner and Forrester, the Inc. 5000, Y Combinator Top Companies, and the Forbes AI 50, Backstory is based in San Francisco and backed by ICONIQ Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Akkadian Ventures and Mubadala Capital.


**The Role**



We are hiring a Senior GTM Enablement Manager to design and scale onboarding and ongoing enablement programs across the go\-to\-market organization. This role ensures every new hire builds strong product and GTM fluency from day one and continues developing expertise as our product and market evolve.



You will partner closely with Product, Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to identify enablement gaps, deliver high\-impact programs, and drive measurable improvements in ramp time, productivity, and pipeline generation.


### **Responsibilities**


* Build and launch a comprehensive onboarding program that all new hires complete within their first two weeks, achieving full completion and strong satisfaction scores
* Create and operationalize a GTM enablement strategy that supports continuous learning through structured training, certifications, and ongoing programming
* Establish a consistent enablement cadence with a defined training calendar, measurable engagement, and successful certification completion across GTM teams
* Partner with GTM and Product teams to assess enablement effectiveness, gather feedback, and translate insights into targeted training and programs
* Build strong cross\-functional alignment with Product and GTM by incorporating feedback loops and deal analysis to continuously improve enablement impact
* Work closely with Product to stay aligned on roadmap, releases, and technical documentation, ensuring all enablement materials remain current and accurate
* Collaborate with Marketing and Product Marketing to maintain competitive intelligence and ensure clear articulation of differentiation in training materials
* Provide regular updates to competitive intelligence and key training materials in partnership with Product Marketing
* Serve as the AI lead for the Marketing team by evaluating tools, developing best practices, and delivering training that improves team efficiency and output
* Drive adoption of AI tools within Marketing through structured evaluations, documented best practices, and high\-impact training sessions
* Develop and scale a dedicated BDR onboarding and enablement program that improves ramp time and increases pipeline generation with strong team feedback
* Contribute to external content initiatives including blog posts, guides, and webinars, ensuring consistent delivery aligned with marketing priorities and campaign timelines
### **Requirements**


* 5 to 7 years of experience in enablement within a high\-growth technology environment
* Experience partnering with Sales, BDR, and Customer Success teams to deliver enablement programs
* Proven track record of building and launching onboarding programs that drive measurable impact
* Strong ability to work cross\-functionally and influence across multiple teams
* Experience working with complex products and technical subject matter
* Excellent attention to detail and strong organizational skills
* Clear and effective communicator, both written and verbal
* Ability to provide structured, actionable feedback and direction
* Strong interest in AI tools and emerging technologies, with experience evaluating and implementing tools that improve productivity
* Strong writing skills with experience contributing to external\-facing content such as blog posts, guides, and thought leadership
* Experience building programs for BDR or SDR teams is preferred
Headquartered in San Francisco, CA, Backstory is backed by Y Combinator and Silicon Valley’s top investors, including ICONIQ Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Akkadian Ventures, and Mubadala Capital. Backstory is listed in the top 20 percent of Inc 5000 companies, is a regular leader in our key categories on G2, and is on the Forbes list of America’s Best Startup Employers. For more information, please visit www.Backstory.ai.
\-
Regular employees may be eligible for commissions or bonus programs (target included in OTE), equity, and benefits. The range is subject to change. Backstory takes into wide range of factors when determining an individual’s pay. These factors can include, skill set, training and experience, licensure and certifications, and location and metropolitan area.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Sales Enablement Manager
description:
At Similarweb, we are revolutionizing the way businesses interact with the digital world by revealing to them everything that happens online. Our unique data and solutions empower over 6,000 customers globally, including industry giants like Google, eBay, and Adidas, to make game\-changing decisions that drive their digital strategies.



In 2021, we went public on the New York Stock Exchange, and we continue to reach new heights! Come work alongside Similarwebbers across the globe who are bright, curious, practical and good people.



We're looking for a **Sales Enablement Manager** to drive go\-to\-market strategy and sales enablement in the Americas region. This role will report to the **Director of Sales Excellence**.


**Why is this role so important at Similarweb?**



Similarweb's digital intelligence solutions serve thousands of customers across many different industries and use cases around the world, and we haven't even scraped the barrel of our total addressable market. As a Sales Excellence Manager for Americas, you will help leadership define the go\-to\-market strategy in the region, and lead execution by leading training, building playbooks and managing various projects to upskill and set up the customer\-facing teams for success.


**So, what will you be doing all day?**


* Work with leadership to define account expansion and net new business strategies, identify operational gaps and opportunities, suggest solutions and prioritize initiatives
* Map skills gaps amongst the GTM team and develop a learning approach to strengthen expertise in those areas
* Collaborate with the regional GM, sales leaders and reps to continuously improve performance by building and implementing rigorous enablement programs and delivering training sessions
* Own a role center of excellence: define what good looks like for Sales Manager or Account Managers, a day in the life, expectations, KPIs and activities for the role
* Lead operational excellence initiatives to improve Ways of Working, build and implement playbooks and manage essential cross\-functional routines within the GTM organization
* Own and lead the global agenda and excellence programs for the region
* Serve as an internal thought leader on the topic of sales excellence and actively contribute to the organization's definition of best practices
* Partner with GTM leadership and management to organize and deliver internal projects, initiatives and events, including our annual GTM Summit


**We are looking for someone who is:**


* An experienced and credible sales person with proven success in target attainment in role discipline, preferably with Enterprise/ Strategic Sales experience closing large complex deals and managing large accounts
* An expert in one or more of the core GTM role disciplines (SDR / Sales / Account Management)
* Excellent problem solver with a passion and high acumen for content design and delivery
* A clear and accurate communicator (both verbal and written), as well as an engaging presenter exhibiting excellent interpersonal skills. Bonus points if you love the stage and it loves you.
* A strategic expert with the skills and ability to drive strategy and enablement end to end \- from analysis and design, to execution, measurement and iteration
* Strong relationship building and stakeholder management skills, ability to work with senior leaders and lead sessions / discussions
* Proactive, self\-starter who can excel in a fast\-paced, highly demanding, and fluid environment
* Worked in a top\-tier management consulting firm \- bonus
* Enablement, sales ops, or sales excellence experience \- bonus


\*\*At Similarweb, collaborating with our colleagues in\-office creates a more connected, unified culture. Our best work is a product of our face\-to\-face collaboration, with the ability to work partially from home after probation period.\*\*



The base salary range for this position in the Burlington, Massachusetts area is $100,000 \- $130,000, plus **benefits including:** medical, dental and vision insurance, 401K plan, potential equity, employee stock purchase plan and paid sick and parental leave.



Individual compensation is based upon a number of factors, including qualifications and relevant experience.



The base salary range above is for the Burlington, Massachusetts area, and could vary for candidates in other locations.


**Why you'll love being a Similarwebber:**  

You'll actually love the product you work with: Our customers aren't our only raving fans. When we asked our employees why they chose to come work at Similarweb, 99% of them said "the product." Imagine how exciting your job is when you get to work with the most powerful digital intelligence platform in the world.


**You'll find a home for your big ideas:** We encourage an open dialogue and empower employees to bring their ideas to the table. You'll find the resources you need to take initiative and create meaningful change within the organization.


**We offer competitive perks \& benefits:** We take your well\-being seriously and offer competitive compensation packages to all employees. We also put a strong emphasis on community, with regular team outings and happy hours.


**You can grow your career in any direction you choose:** Interested in becoming a VP or want to transition into a different department? Whether it's Career Week, personalized coaching, or our ongoing learning solutions, you'll find all the tools and opportunities you need to develop your career right here.


**Diversity isn't just a buzzword:** People want to work in a place where they can be themselves. We strive to create a workplace that is reflective of the communities we serve, where everyone is empowered to bring their full, authentic selves to work. We are committed to inclusivity across race, gender, ethnicity, culture, sexual orientation, age, religion, spirituality, identity, and experience. We believe our culture of equality and mutual respect also helps us better understand and serve our customers in a world that is becoming more global, more diverse, and more digital every day.


***Please Note: We are unable to sponsor Visas at this time.***


***\#LI\-NP \#LI\-Hybrid***

  


We will handle your application and information related to your application in accordance with the Applicant Privacy Policy available here.

title: EverCommerce - Talent Acquisition Sourcer & Enablement Specialist
description:
At EverCommerce \[Nasdaq: EVCM], we are on a mission to digitally transform the service economy with tailored, end\-to\-end SaaS solutions that simplify and empower the lives of our 745,000\+ customers. As a leading service commerce platform, our modern digital and mobile applications create predictable, informed, and convenient experiences between customers and their service professionals in the areas of Home \& Field Services, Health Services, and Wellness industries.


We are building an extraordinary company and looking for talented, energetic, and motivated people to join our team. You can learn more about our Company, Culture and Values here: https://careers.evercommerce.com/us/en


We are looking for a Talent Acquisition Sourcer \& Enablement Specialist to focus on our Talent Acquisition function. Our Talent Acquisition team is responsible for attracting, engaging, and hiring top talent across EverCommerce’s global organization. We leverage modern tools, data\-driven strategies, and innovative talent marketing approaches to build strong pipelines and deliver an exceptional candidate experience.


We are seeking a dynamic and proactive Talent Acquisition Sourcer \& Enablement Specialist to join our Talent Acquisition team. This hybrid role blends strategic sourcing with talent marketing and recruitment enablement. You will play a critical role in building and nurturing talent pipelines, elevating our employer brand, and optimizing candidate engagement through our Phenom platform.


This role will report into Talent Acquisition leadership and partner closely with recruiters, hiring managers, and cross\-functional stakeholders.

**Responsibilities**

* Proactively source and engage passive candidates across key talent segments
* Build and maintain strong, diverse talent pipelines aligned to current and future hiring needs
* Collaborate with Talent Acquisition Partners to develop sourcing strategies for hard\-to\-fill and high\-priority roles
* Own and manage talent pipelines within LinkedIn Recruiter, Phenom CRM, and other applicable sourcing tools, ensuring data integrity and effective segmentation
* Build and execute targeted CRM campaigns to nurture and engage candidates
* Support and execute talent marketing initiatives to attract top talent
* Collaborate with internal stakeholders to develop compelling job ads, campaigns, and messaging
* Maintain and update the company career site using Phenom CMS, ensuring content accuracy and alignment with employer brand standards
* Monitor and analyze pipeline health, campaign performance, and sourcing effectiveness
* Identify opportunities to improve candidate experience, sourcing strategies, and TA processes
* Support enablement efforts by documenting best practices and improving TA tools and workflows

**Skills and Experience needed for success in this role**

* 5\-7\+ years of experience in talent acquisition, sourcing, recruiting operations, or a related field
* Experience with CRM tools (Phenom strongly preferred) and sourcing platforms
* Experience with the Workday HRIS platform is preferred
* Strong understanding of sourcing strategies and candidate engagement techniques
* Experience with talent marketing, employer branding, or recruitment campaigns
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment
* Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and optimize performance
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills
* Experience with career site management or CMS platforms is a plus
* Familiarity with TA enablement, operations, or process improvement is preferred

**Where: Remote, US**


The EverCommerce team is distributed globally, with teams across multiple regions. This role can be based anywhere in the United States – if you’re close to one of our offices, we can set you up in\-office or you can work 100% remotely. Please note that you must be eligible to work without sponsorship to qualify for this position, and this role may require occasional travel to our Corporate Headquarters in Denver, Colorado, or to other office locations within the United States.

**Benefits and Perks**

* Flexibility to work where/how you want within the United States – in\-office, remote, or hybrid
* Day 1 access to robust health and wellness benefits, including an annual wellness stipend
* 401k with up to a 4% match and immediate vesting
* Flexible and generous (FTO) time\-off
* Employee Stock Purchase Program

**Compensation**


The target base compensation for this position is **$80,000 to $95,000 USD** per year in most U.S. locations. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors including location, local market variances, and candidate experience and expertise, and may vary from the amounts listed above.


EverCommerce is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We look forward to reviewing your credentials and getting to know more about your experience!

title: Senior Sales Enablement Specialist (Hybrid)
description:
**Hi, Future Homie!**


At Homebase, you’ll join a team that’s bold, fast\-moving, and obsessed with helping small businesses thrive. We build with empathy, act with urgency, and take big swings that drive real\-world impact. Here, every Homie shows up to raise the bar, support one another, and celebrate wins as a team.


We’re not just building an app—we’re building unstoppable teams. So what do you say, are you in?

 **Your** ***Impact*** **Starts Here**


We’re hiring a **Senior Sales Enablement Specialist** to help build and scale enablement across our sales organization. This is an individual contributor (IC) role designed for someone ready to take the next step in ownership and impact.


You'll work closely with our Senior Sales Enablement Manager and be the boots\-on\-the\-ground presence for our Houston sales team. This is a hands\-on builder role: you'll create, deliver, and iterate on training while identifying gaps, maintaining our tools, and helping us scale.


If you love building programs that actually change behavior (not just sit in a deck), this role is for you.

**These are the key ways you’ll contribute and create impact in this role:**

**Onboarding \& Continuous Learning**

* Own day\-to\-day execution of new hire onboarding: managing schedules, calendar invites, and ensuring all content is loaded and ready
* Track engagement \& completion of enablement materials, grade evaluations, and surface knowledge gaps so we can close them fast
* Develop and deliver virtual, in\-person, and self\-paced training sessions tailored to individual sales roles across our payroll, core, and hiring product lines
* Maintain and update onboarding materials as our product, sales process, and tools evolve

**Content Development**

* Partner with our Senior Manager on training priorities and content development: training decks, Kahoots, talk tracks, one\-pagers, demo guides, role\-play practice tools, and microlearning modules
* Translate product updates, new partner announcements, and GTM strategy/process changes into clear, seller\-ready enablement assets
* Create and maintain a centralized repository of all sales enablement materials, ensuring content is current, easy to find, and mapped to the right use case and audience
* Apply instructional design principles, multimodal delivery, engagement activities, reinforcement loops, and impact measurement to ensure content actually sticks

**Sales Tools \& Technology**

* Serve as a day\-to\-day power user and subject matter expert on our sales tech stack (e.g. Salesforce, Talkdesk, CI platform, and AI tools such as our GPT knowledge base)
* Prompt and configure our CI tool to surface rep skill gaps, objection handling trends, and coaching plans/opportunities
* Utilize Salesforce reporting and pipeline data to identify seller skill gaps or process improvements

**Sales Performance \& Cross\-Functional Collaboration**

* Partner with sales managers to absorb coaching requests and translate them into scalable training resources
* Reinforce sales methodologies and best practices through ongoing sessions, deal support, and coaching materials
* Collaborate with Product Marketing, RevOps, and Sales Leadership to stay ahead of what's changing and translate it into the field quickly
* Partner with internal stakeholders and external selling teams where needed to design \& deliver training beyond the core sales org

**The Foundation for Success \-** These are the experiences and strengths that will set you up for success in this role:

* 2\+ years in sales enablement, sales training, L\&D, or a closely related field OR meaningful sales experience (AE, SDR) with a demonstrated pivot into enablement or training work.
* Hands\-on experience with an LMS platform (e.g., Lessonly, Mindtickle, Allego, Highspot, or similar)
* Hands\-on experience with a conversation intelligence tool (e.g., Gong, Jiminny, Clari, Chorus, LevelAI, Nooks, or similar)
* Proficiency in Salesforce (e.g., report building, dashboard familiarity, pipeline monitoring)
* Experience building seller\-facing content (e.g., talk tracks, training decks, one\-pagers, training modules, or playbooks)
* Strong facilitation skills (e.g., commanding a room, gamification, interactive presentation skills)
* Proven experience in prompting AI tools to improve workflows, generate content, and extract insights

**The Homie Way \-** These principles guide everything we do—from how we work and make decisions to how we show up for each other.

* **Be Customer Obsessed** – Solve problems with empathy and creativity.
* **Move Fast, Learn Fast** – Experiment, take action, and grow every day.
* **Own Your Impact** – Think big, focus on what matters, and make decisions you stand behind.
* **Master Your Craft** – Excellence fuels impact—show up, step up, and make your mark.
* **Win Together** – Put goals over roles, lead with trust, and connect to our mission and each other.

**What We Offer**

* Ownership \& Financial Security: Stock options \+ 401(k) with 4% match
* Comprehensive Healthcare: Medical, dental, and vision coverage \+ FSA options
* Flexible Time: Unlimited PTO (salaried) \+ company holidays
* AI access, for real: We invest in builders and believe that curiosity shouldn't have a paywall. That means you'll have access to paid AI tools with minimal restrictions, so you can build, experiment, and level up your craft.
* Family Support: Up to 12 weeks of paid parental leave (after 6 months of service)
* Protection Plans: Life insurance \+ short/long\-term disability coverage
* Work Your Way: Work From Anywhere Month \+ meeting\-free weeks yearly
* Workspace Perks: Meals provided, commuter benefits, team offsites, and Customer Days
* Our Hybrid Rhythm: We believe collaboration drives impact. That’s why **Monday \- Thursday are our required in\-office days**—a time to move faster as a team, build deeper connections, make better decisions, and build together.

 **Belonging at Homebase \-** We're committed to fostering a welcoming space where every Homie can be their full self. Experience comes in many forms—so if you're excited about this role, even if you don’t meet 100% of the qualifications, we encourage you to apply!

*Homebase will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.*

**Hey, We’re Homebase**


Homebase is the everything app for hourly teams—built to simplify the day\-to\-day and superpower local businesses. With tools for scheduling, time clocks, payroll, communication, HR, and more, we help teams stay connected and in control. Today, over 150,000 small (but mighty) businesses rely on us to make work radically easier. Together, we’ve tracked over a billion hours for 2M\+ workers—and we’re just getting started.


At Homebase, we celebrate diversity and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We welcome all candidates and do not discriminate based on any legally protected status. If you need accommodations during the hiring process, please let us know—we’re committed to ensuring fair and equitable access for all.

title: Product Operations Enablement Manager – Vice President
description:
**JOB DESCRIPTION**  

Join the Consumer and Community Banking (CCB) Product \& Experience and Technology (PXT) organization as we continue on a multi\-year journey to refine our Product Operating Model, transitioning from a highly standardized, waterfall project\-based operating model into a decentralized Agile model with over 100 autonomous products. Our largest priorities impact multiple products, portfolios, and lines of business which require more consistent ways of working across the organization. We are building a team to enable these new ways of working at scale by focusing on the adoption of necessary processes and supporting tooling solutions, in addition to leading the execution against these priorities in some cases.




As a Product Operations Enablement Manager – Vice President within the CCB Product Operations organization, you will be responsible for leading an enablement team to either teach the organization how to execute new scaled ways of working or, in some cases, lead the execution to facilitate the delivery of our largest strategic priorities. You will be directly responsible for a team of coaches and subject matter experts, each aligned to strategic priorities and portfolios.


You will be a visionary leader of character, with a proven ability to manage teams, influence executive stakeholders, and align diverse groups toward common goals. Additionally, you will drive cultural and behavioral change at scale by introducing new ways of working, coaching large organizations through adoption, and inspiring confidence in new changes through facilitation. You have an understanding of strategic vision, operational expertise, and a customer\-centric approach to meet teams where they are operationally and guide them towards continuous improvement as they deliver the organization’s strategic goals.

**Job Responsibilities**

* Teach large program teams how to execute Discovery, Planning, and Delivery across products and lines of business
* Drive integration and connectivity across products and portfolios, breaking down silos, and fostering collaboration across the organization
* Lead a team of coaches and facilitators responsible for teaching teams new ways of working across a matrixed organization, shifting the organization from disparate ways of working to value\-aligned thinking and execution
* Champion and lead adoption of enterprise\-wide change initiatives, driving adoption of new PDLC best practices, processes, tools, and ways of working across all impacted teams and portfolios
* Observe and share opportunities to continuously improve operational frameworks, governance structures, and tooling solutions to optimize efficiency and effectiveness at scale
* Serve as a trusted advisor to program and product leadership, providing strategic insights, transparent reporting, and proactive risk management. Communicate context, strategy, and value proposition to inspire motivation and alignment
* Build, mentor, and inspire a high\-performing team, nurturing a culture of integrity, accountability, inclusion, and continuous improvement
* Oversee the identification, escalation, and resolution of risks and issues that may impact program or portfolio performance, ensuring blockers are addressed

**Required Qualifications, skills, and capabilities:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Technology, or related field
* 6\+ years of experience in product management, program management, or portfolio operations within a large, matrixed organization
* Experience with large\-scale Agile and/or Product Operating Model transformations
* Expertise in multiple delivery frameworks (e.g., Scrum, Kanban, SaFE, Lean, Theory of Constraints, Product Development Flow, etc.)
* Deep expertise in Product Development Lifecycle methodologies, operational frameworks, and governance
* Experience as a key contributor in a product development role (e.g., product manager, software engineer, designer, etc.)
* Demonstrated experience managing multiple teams and large\-scale, cross\-functional programs impacting multiple products or portfolios
* Proven track record of leading teams, developing talent, and driving organizational transformation
* Exceptional executive communication, stakeholder management, and strategic problem\-solving skills
* Ability to influence, align, and rally large, diverse groups toward common enterprise goals

**Preferred Qualifications, skills, and capabilities:**

* Experience in financial services or a highly regulated industry
* Familiarity with banking, finance, and digital experience products, platforms, and organizational structure
* Advanced data analysis, reporting, and executive presentation skills

**Additional Information**

* *This role requires the ability to physically work in our offices 5 days a week*
* *Applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S. We are unable to provide immigration sponsorship or take over sponsorship of an employment/work visa at this time (including but not limited to H1B, H4 – EAD, OPT, TN, or L visas)*
* *Final Job Grade level and corporate title will be determined at time of offer and may differ from this posting*
* *This role does not provide relocation assistance so all candidates must be local to the work locations listed in the job posting or willing to relocate on their own immediately upon hiring*


**ABOUT US**  

Chase is a leading financial services firm, helping nearly half of America’s households and small businesses achieve their financial goals through a broad range of financial products. Our mission is to create engaged, lifelong relationships and put our customers at the heart of everything we do. We also help small businesses, nonprofits and cities grow, delivering solutions to solve all their financial needs.


We offer a competitive total rewards package including base salary determined based on the role, experience, skill set and location. Those in eligible roles may receive commission\-based pay and/or discretionary incentive compensation, paid in the form of cash and/or forfeitable equity, awarded in recognition of individual achievements and contributions. We also offer a range of benefits and programs to meet employee needs, based on eligibility. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage, on\-site health and wellness centers, a retirement savings plan, backup childcare, tuition reimbursement, mental health support, financial coaching and more. Additional details about total compensation and benefits will be provided during the hiring process.


We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.


Equal Opportunity Employer/Disability/Veterans

  

  

  

**ABOUT THE TEAM**  


Our Consumer \& Community Banking division serves our Chase customers through a range of financial services, including personal banking, credit cards, mortgages, auto financing, investment advice, small business loans and payment processing. We’re proud to lead the U.S. in credit card sales and deposit growth and have the most\-used digital solutions – all while ranking first in customer satisfaction.  

  

The Digital team is dedicated to creating innovative, industry\-leading products and experiences that help customers access, share and control their financial data so they can make smart decisions with their money. Teams enable innovation while adhering to the firm’s data sharing principles of security, customer control and convenience, and privacy.

title: Senior GTM Enablement Specialist
description:
**About Lyra Health**

Lyra Health is the leading provider of mental health solutions for employers supporting more than 20 million people globally. The company has delivered 13 million sessions of mental health care, published more than 20 peer\-reviewed studies, and delivered unmatched outcomes in terms of access, clinical effectiveness and cost efficiency. Extensive peer\-reviewed research confirms Lyra’s transformative care model helps people recover twice as fast and results in a 26% annual reduction in overall healthcare claims costs. Lyra is transforming access to life\-changing mental health care through Lyra Empower, the only fully integrated, AI\-powered platform combining the highest\-quality care and technology solutions.  

Lyra Health is seeking a Senior GTM Enablement Specialist to join our dynamic GTM Enablement team, with a strong focus on content creation, program design, and live facilitation. This is a hands\-on GTM Enablement position where you will be directly responsible for developing and delivering enablement programs that empower our Sales and Customer Success teams to drive exceptional value for our prospects and customers.



In this role, you will be the bridge between GTM strategy and field execution. You will design engaging learning journeys and facilitate live sessions that empower our teams to communicate Lyra’s value. You are someone who loves the craft of enablement, building the story, coaching the rep, and using cutting\-edge AI to make those experiences more personalized and effective.



### **Responsibilities**

**Enablement Program Design, Creation, Facilitation, Tracking and Execution:**


* Design, execute, deliver, and continuously improve onboarding, ramping, and ongoing skill development programs for GTM teams, ensuring alignment with the broader GTM Enablement strategy.
* **Optimize Revenue Intelligence:** Manage and optimize **Gong** to identify winning behaviors, track initiative adoption, and create "Gold Standard" libraries for the GTM org.
* **AI\-Driven Coaching:** Integrate AI tools like **Yoodli** into enablement experiences to provide scalable, automated role\-play and communication coaching for market\-facing teams.
* **Operational Efficiency with AI:** Consistently leverage AI productivity tools to accelerate your own workflow, from content drafting and video editing to data analysis and program synthesis.
* Develop, track, and oversee progress of assessments, certifications, and measurable outcomes for enablement programs, leveraging insights to make data\-driven improvements.
* Collaborate with the GTM Enablement team and cross\-functional subject matter experts (SMEs) to create and deliver engaging content, including communications, live facilitation, templates, and playbooks.
* Curate and maintain multi\-modality learning content within our LMS and enablement platform, ensuring accessibility and alignment with team needs.
* Contribute to the planning and facilitation of large\-scale initiatives, such as annual kickoffs (SKO) and product launches.

**Stakeholder Management:**


* Partner with main stakeholders to assess enablement needs and translate them into actionable, impactful plans that drive team performance.
* Support the collection and reporting efforts for enablement programs, providing insights and recommendations to optimize initiatives and accelerate revenue growth.
### **Qualifications**


* **Experience:** 3\+ years in Sales, Customer Success, or GTM Enablement
* **Full\-Cycle Enablement:** Proven ability to build programs from scratch, taking them from initial design and content creation to live facilitation and coaching.
* **Dynamic Facilitation:** Comfortable commanding a in person or virtual room and adapting your style for everyone from new hires to seasoned GTM ICs and leaders.
* **Tech Stack:** Proficiency with **CRM, Enablement Platforms and LMS** to organize knowledge and track impact.
* **Revenue Intelligence :** Experience using revenue intelligence tools to identify winning behaviors and turn call insights into actionable coaching strategies.
* **AI Fluency \& Curiosity:** Active use of AI tools (or willingness to learn) to accelerate your own productivity and a passion for teaching GTM teams how to do the same.
* **Strategic Action:** A "builder" mindset with high\-impact execution of strategic planning, with the ability to manage multiple projects based on business needs.
* **Empathy\-Led Design:** Deep understanding of GTM pain points, with the ability to translate stakeholder needs into simple, effective enablement solutions.
* **Domain Knowledge:** Experience in HR, Benefits, or Healthcare is a plus.
As a full\-time Sr. GTM Enablement Specialist, you will be employed by Lyra Health, Inc. The anticipated annual base salary range for this full\-time position is $95,000\-$131,000\. The base range is determined by role and level, and placement within the range will depend on a number of job\-related factors, including but not limited to your skills, qualifications, experience and location. This role may also be eligible for discretionary bonuses.  

  

Annual salary is only one part of an employee’s total compensation package at Lyra. We also offer generous benefits that include:  

* Comprehensive healthcare coverage (including medical, dental, vision, FSA/HSA, life and disability insurances)
* Lyra for Lyrians; coaching and therapy services
* Equity in the company through discretionary restricted stock units
* Competitive time off with pay policies including vacation, sick days, and company holidays
* Paid parental leave
* 401K retirement benefits

* We like to spread joy throughout the year with well\-being perks and activities, surprise swag, regular community celebration…and more!

  

We can’t wait to meet you

title: Sales Enablement Coach
description:
**Sales Enablement Coach**

**North\- Remote**

***Let’s go North, together.***


Join our mission to build the largest suite of credit card processing and merchant services. It’s one simple payment platform backed by the most diverse payment companies. From credit card processing to back\-office management, North points the way to smarter, faster, and just plain better payment solutions.

**Job Summary:**  

As the Sales Enablement Coach, you will take ownership of the sales\-specific development and ongoing enablement of our North Direct Sales team. Following the initial corporate onboarding provided by our L\&D team, you will step in to equip sales representatives with the specific skills, product knowledge, and tactical strategies needed to succeed. This role is responsible for driving ongoing coaching programs, developing standard operating procedures (SOPs) for selling new products, and managing internal communications to ensure our sales teams are always prepared to go to market effectively.

 **What you'll do:*** Design and execute comprehensive, sales\-specific development programs for new hires outside of their general L\&D onboarding.
* Manage the intake process for new Internal product announcements and create battlecards for sales teams to easily ingest the new product details and onboarding requirements.
* Manage internal communications regarding product updates, competitive intelligence, and market shifts to ensure the sales team is informed.
* Conduct continuous one\-on\-one and group coaching sessions focusing on call reviews, objection handling, pitch refinement, and closing techniques.
* Collaborate with sales and cross\-functional departments to identify, report and solve process and communication gaps.
* Provide a high\-touch, proactive experience for our internal sales teams to ensure we are actively improving sales processes and systems.
* Assist in developing and documenting processes/procedures for selling newly launched products.
 **Education and Experience:**  

* High School Diploma or equivalent required
* 3\+ years of experience in B2B sales required, within the payments space preferred
* Prior experience in sales coaching, enablement, or team lead capacity.
* 2\+ years of experience with sales tools such as (but not limited to): Hubspot, Outreach, Seamless.ai, Zoom Info, Google Suite, VOIP solutions.
**What we offer:**


We offer a comprehensive benefits package that enables our teams to live a *life well lived,* both personally and professionally. Some of our perks include:

* Medical, Dental, \& Vision Coverage
* Flexible Paid Time Off
* 401(k) \+ Match
* Education Assistance
* Paid Maternity \& Paternity Leave
* Mental Health Support \& Well\-Being Program
* Company\-funded Lifestyle Spending Account

**Salary:** $75,000 \+ quarterly bonus


Pay within this range varies by work location and on job\-related knowledge, skills, and experience. We look forward to discussing your salary expectations and our full total rewards offerings throughout the interview process.

*Please note: North American Bancard is a US\-based company and no sponsorship is available for this position at this time.*

**Who we are:**


North, and our family of companies, are committed to helping entrepreneurs grow their businesses. As an end\-to\-end payment solutions company, we provide everything business owners need to get paid, whether they serve customers in a physical storefront, online, or both. We pride ourselves on being large enough to offer customized solutions to our enterprise\-level clients while remaining agile enough to take an award\-winning, hands\-on approach to personal service that our merchants won’t find anywhere else.


Let’s go North, together! Our most important resource is our people. Join our diverse team of innovators and do\-ers and make your mark on the future of payments technology. We're proud to offer benefits that help our team members further their overall well\-being through unique initiatives that are both personally and professionally fulfilling.


At North, we celebrate diversity and create an inclusive environment for everyone. We are an equal opportunity employer.

*To learn more about North, and our family of companies, visit our website:* north.com

At Vanta, our mission is to help businesses earn and prove trust.We believe that security should be monitored and verified continuously, and we empower companies to practice better security and prove it with ease. Vanta has a kind and talented team, and while some have prior security experience, many have been successful at Vanta without it.
As the first Sr. Product Enablement Manager at Vanta, you'll be the architect of how our GTM teams learn, understand, and articulate Vanta's product value and capabilities to customers. You'll design and deliver enablement programs that translate product strategy into field readiness, drive product adoption, and directly impact revenue outcomes.


This is a senior IC role that will own the full lifecycle of product enablement for both presales and post sales GTM — from discovery and curriculum design, rigorous program management, through delivery, measurement, and continuous iteration. You'll partner closely with PMM, Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to ensure that every team member can confidently sell, implement, and expand Vanta with our customers.

**What you’ll do as a Senior Product Enablement Manager at Vanta:**

* Develop the Product Enablement Strategy: Define and execute a comprehensive product enablement and training strategy aligned with product launches, business goals, and customer needs.
* Create and Manage Training Content: Lead the creation of engaging and scalable training content, including video tutorials, e\-learning modules, webinars, and certifications.
* Oversee Product Documentation: Partner with product teams to ensure our help center, knowledge base, and other documentation are accurate, easy to understand, and up\-to date.
* Analyze and Optimize: Use data and feedback to measure the effectiveness of training programs and documentation. Continuously iterate and improve the user learning experience.
* Serve as a Subject Matter Expert: Act as a go\-to resource for customer\-facing teams (sales, support, customer success) by developing internal enablement materials and delivering training sessions.
* Collaborate Cross\-Functionally: Work closely with product managers and PMMs to understand new features and roadmap plans, and with marketing and sales to support go\-to\-market strategies.
* Define and track success metrics (time\-to\-product proficiency, adoption rates, feature utilization, customer expansion, NPS impact) and present data\-driven insights to leadership
* Stay current on product changes, competitive landscape, and industry trends; proactively identify opportunities to improve enablement quality
* Experiment with product knowledge testing through innovative AI approaches

**How to be successful in this role:**

* 8–10 years in product enablement, GTM enablement, or similar role; bonus points for prior quota\-carrying sales or customer success or PMM experience
* Expertise in curriculum design and adult learning principles; ability to create engaging, relevant, and results\-oriented training programs
* Product acumen—ability to quickly understand complex product features, positioning, and differentiation, and translate them into field\-ready training
* Proficiency with enablement technology including LMS platforms (Sana), content management systems (Seismic, Highspot), knowledge bases (Guru), and conversation intelligence tools (Gong)
* Strong measurement and data\-driven mindset; ability to define KPIs, track enablement ROI, and use data to drive decision\-making and continuous improvement
* Excellent cross\-functional collaboration skills with ability to build relationships, drive influence, and align diverse stakeholders around shared goals
* Strong communication and presentation skills; ability to simplify complexity and engage diverse audiences
* Customer\-first mindset with a systems thinking approach; you design enablement with learner needs and real\-world application at the center
* Huge plus if you are familiar with the GRC/Security space or have worked in a highly technical environment.
* Open to using AI to amplify their skills and strengthen their work \- demonstrating curiosity, a willingness to learn, and sound judgment in applying AI responsibly to improve efficiency and impact.
* Must be authorized to work in the U.S. without the need for current or future employer sponsorship.

**What you can expect as a Vanta’n:**

* Industry\-competitive salary and equity
* Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage, with 100% of employee\-only benefit premiums covered for most medical plans
* 16 weeks paid Parental Leave for all new parents
* Health \& wellness stipend
* Remote workspace, internet, and cellphone stipend
* Commuter benefits for team members who report to the SF and NYC office
* Family planning benefits
* Matching 401(k) contribution with immediate vesting
* Flexible PTO policy, plus 80 hours of Sick Time
* 11 company\-paid holidays
* Virtual team building activities, lunch and learns, and other company\-wide events!
* Offices in SF, NYC, London, Dublin, Tel Aviv, and Sydney


To provide greater transparency to candidates, we share base pay ranges for all US\-based job postings regardless of state. We set standard base pay ranges for all roles based on function, level, and country location, benchmarked against similar\-stage growth companies. Final offer amounts are determined by multiple factors and may vary based on candidate location, skills, depth of work experience, and relevant licenses/credentials.


\#LI\-remote

*At Vanta, we are committed to hiring diverse talent of different backgrounds and as such, it is important to us to provide an inclusive work environment for all. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender identity, age, religion, sexual orientation, veteran or disability status, or any other protected class. As an equal opportunity employer, we encourage and welcome people of all backgrounds to apply.*

**About Vanta**


We started in 2018, in the wake of several high\-profile data breaches. Online security was only becoming more important, but we knew firsthand how hard it could be for fast\-growing companies to invest the time and manpower it takes to build a solid security foundation. Vanta was inspired by a vision to restore trust in internet businesses by enabling companies to improve and prove their security. From our early days automating security monitoring for compliance standards like SOC 2, HIPAA and ISO 27001 to creating the world's leading Trust Management Platform, our vision remains unchanged.


Now more than ever, making security continuous—not just a point\-in\-time check— is essential. Thousands of companies rely on Vanta to build, maintain and demonstrate their trust— all in a way that's real\-time and transparent.

**Referral Instructions**


If you are being referred for the role, please contact that person to apply on your behalf.

title: Applied Epic Workflow & Enablement Lead
description:
**Role Summary**

We’re seeking a hands‑on Applied Epic Expert to own the design, optimization, and enablement of agency workflows that scale. This role combines process architecture, reporting \& analytics, training \& change management, and data quality stewardship to deliver consistent, high‑quality customer experience across the business unit. You will serve as the Subject Matter Expert (SME) for Applied Epic across Operations and Training \& Development, and act as a thought leader in how we leverage the platform to drive growth, efficiency, and retention.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Workflow Strategy \& Design**

* Analyze current end‑to‑end workflows in Applied Epic; map pain points, handoffs, SLAs, and system dependencies.
* Design scalable, standardized workflows (personal/commercial lines, renewals, endorsements, billing, service, and carrier interactions) that balance efficiency, compliance, and customer experience.
* Build clear SOPs, swim lanes, and role‑based playbooks; maintain a governed library of process assets.

**Platform Optimization \& Data Quality**

* Lead ongoing Applied Epic assessment and optimization (configuration, permissions, fields, layouts, activity codes, and integrations) to reduce manual effort and eliminate defects.
* Define and enforce data standards (naming conventions, required fields, validation rules); establish monitoring to improve data completeness, accuracy, and timeliness.
* Partner with IT/Vendor teams to prioritize and deliver enhancements including automation and AI enablement; perform UAT and change rollout.

**Reporting, Analytics \& Insights**

* Build and maintain Applied Epic reports and dashboards that surface operational KPIs (retention, cycle time, workload, backlog, E\&O risk indicators, premium/commission trends).
* Translate data into actionable insights and recommendations for leaders and frontline teams.
* Create self‑service reporting guides and data dictionaries to scale access and understanding.

**Training, Enablement \& Change Management**

* Develop role‑based training curricula (new hire, upskilling, refreshers) on Applied Epic, workflows, and best practices.
* Deliver engaging live and on‑demand sessions; author step‑by‑step guides, quick reference cards, and micro‑videos.
* Drive adoption using change plans, stakeholder mapping, communication calendars, and success metrics.

**Cross‑Functional Leadership \& Governance**

* Serve as the SME for Applied Epic across the business unit—advising Operations, Training \& Development, Client Engagement, Finance, and Leadership on platform capabilities and process improvements.
* Facilitate a workflow governance forum; prioritize the roadmap, measure impact, and continuously iterate.
* Coordinate with vendors and carriers as needed for escalations, integrations, and best‑practice alignment.

**Qualifications Required**

* 4–7\+ years working with Applied Epic in an insurance agency/brokerage environment.
* Proven experience designing and documenting workflows and SOPs that scale across teams.
* Strong reporting/analytics skills in Applied Epic (plus Excel/Power BI or equivalent).
* Demonstrated training and enablement capability: curriculum design, facilitation, and adoption.
* Solid grasp of P\&C insurance operations (renewals, endorsements, certificates, billing, downloads, suspense, and compliance).
* Excellent communication skills; ability to translate complex concepts into clear, actionable guidance.

**Preferred**

* Experience leading data quality initiatives; familiarity with data governance and stewardship.
* Hands‑on change management experience
* Exposure to adjacent systems (download, rating, CRM, automation platforms) and API/integration basics.
* Insurance designations (e.g., CIC, CPCU, CISR) a plus.

**KPI’s**

* **Operational Efficiency:** Reduced cycle time, rework, and touches per transaction.
* **Adoption \& Competency:** Training completion, proficiency scores, and workflow compliance.
* **Data Quality:** Improvements in field completeness/accuracy, reduction in suspense/exception queues.
* **Business Outcomes:** Higher retention/NPS, improved E\&O posture, and visibility via standardized dashboards.
* **Enablement Impact:** Time‑to‑productivity for new hires; utilization of self‑service assets.

Pay: From $85,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* 401(k) matching
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Remote

title: Sales Enablement Manager, Account Management
description:

This role will collaborate closely with internal stakeholders to align enablement strategies with business objectives, ensuring all sales priorities are fully supported.



Design, implement, and manage comprehensive sales enablement and training programs. Ensure that all programs align with corporate goals and drive sales objectives by accelerating sales productivity and enhancing the knowledge, skills, and competencies of Zywave Account Managers.

title: AI Enablement Specialist
description:
Description:  



**CUMBERLAND HEIGHTS**


We are looking for you! Do you want to be a part of the team that transforms lives? Cumberland Heights is more than a campus on 177 acres, it’s a community. Our mission is providing the highest quality care possible for persons and families who are at risk for or who are suffering from the disease of chemical dependency. Treatment encompasses the physical, mental, emotional, and spiritual dimensions of recovery by offering a safe, loving and healing environment, combining professional excellence and the principles of the Twelve Steps.

**Cumberland Heights Foundation offers a comprehensive benefits program, which includes:**

* Medical, Dental and Vision effective 1st day of month following 28 days of employment
* Employer Contribution for Health Saving Account or Health Reimbursement Account
* 401K with Company match and eligibility after 90 days of employment
* Paid Time Off (PTO) accrual beginning day (1\) one and up to 20 days of PTO the first year

**POSITION SUMMARY**


Cumberland Heights is a nationally recognized addiction treatment organization, and the technology that supports our clinical teams directly impacts patient outcomes. We are at an inflection point: AI and automation tools available to healthcare organizations today have the potential to meaningfully reduce administrative friction and improve how our staff work. Realizing that potential requires someone who can bridge the gap between what is possible and what is practical.


The AI Enablement Specialist is a new role created to do exactly that. This person is the primary executor of Cumberland Heights’ AI roadmap. They work within a purposefully designed resource model: the CSO provides strategic direction, a two\-person internal development team provides technical build capacity, and a contracted AI strategy consultant provides senior architectural guidance during complex build phases. The AI Enablement Specialist is the connective tissue — translating organizational needs into scoped projects, coordinating across all three resources, and ensuring that pilots move from idea to production.


Equally important, this person builds the cross\-functional relationships and project discipline that make adoption stick. AI tools only create value when people trust and use them. This role owns that challenge.

**AI Use Case Identification \& Piloting**

* Conduct structured discovery with clinical, operational, and administrative departments to identify high\-value AI and automation opportunities
* Build and maintain a prioritized use case pipeline using a consistent evaluation framework (impact, feasibility, compliance, and cost)
* Lead pilots from scoping through production deployment, coordinating between internal developers, the AI strategy consultant, and department stakeholders
* Document pilot outcomes, lessons learned, and scalability assessments

**Technology Roadmap Execution**

* Serve as the operational owner of Cumberland Heights’ AI roadmap, tracking progress against approved initiatives and flagging risks or delays
* Partner with the CSO to translate organizational strategy into scoped, sequenced projects
* Coordinate vendor evaluations and build\-versus\-buy analyses, presenting findings in plain language for executive decision\-making
* Monitor the healthcare AI vendor market and bring forward relevant opportunities on a defined cadence

**Staff Training \& Adoption**

* Design and deliver AI literacy training and hands\-on workshops for staff across departments and roles
* Create role\-specific guidance documents and job aids that make AI tools accessible to non\-technical staff
* Serve as a primary internal resource for questions about approved AI tools, including the organization’s ChatGPT Enterprise environment
* Identify and develop internal AI champions within departments to sustain adoption between formal training cycles

**Governance \& Compliance**

* Ensure all AI initiatives are implemented in accordance with the organization’s AI Acceptable Use Policy and HIPAA requirements
* Maintain documentation of active AI tools, integrations, data access patterns, and approval status
* Participate in governance reviews and flag emerging compliance considerations as new tools or capabilities are introduced
* Uphold the organization’s explicit boundary: AI supports and removes friction from administrative work; it does not replace or augment clinical judgment

**Project Coordination \& Stakeholder Management**

* Maintain a project management discipline across all active AI initiatives, including clear owners, timelines, and status reporting
* Serve as the primary liaison between IT, the development team, the AI strategy consultant, and department leads
* Communicate progress, risks, and decisions in formats appropriate to the audience — from IT technical staff to executive leadership
* Provide regular status reporting to the CSO on roadmap progress, active pilots, and emerging risks, and participate in executive\-level reviews as requested
* Build and maintain trusted relationships across the organization as the face of AI enablement


Requirements:
* Bachelors degree in information technology, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field required
* 3\+ years of experience in a technology, operations, or project management role with direct exposure to AI or automation tools
* Demonstrated ability to manage cross\-functional projects from scoping through delivery
* Strong communication skills — comfortable translating technical concepts for non\-technical audiences and organizational needs for technical teams
* Familiarity with enterprise AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or comparable platforms) and practical experience applying them in a business context
* Working knowledge of data privacy principles and compliance considerations relevant to AI deployment

**Preferred**

* Experience in healthcare, behavioral health, or other regulated environments (HIPAA experience a strong plus)
* Familiarity with prompt engineering, LLM evaluation, or AI governance frameworks
* Experience designing or delivering technology training for non\-technical staff
* Relevant certifications or coursework in AI, project management (PMP, CAPM), or healthcare IT

**Measurable Outcomes**

* Roadmap execution: Active AI pilots progressing on schedule with documented outcomes
* Adoption: Departments using approved AI tools with measurable reduction in manual effort
* Staff capability: AI literacy workshops delivered; internal champions identified and active
* Governance: All active AI tools documented, approved, and compliant with AUP and HIPAA
* Stakeholder confidence: Department leaders view this role as a trusted resource, not a technology imposition

title: TECHNOLOGY INTEGRATION & ENABLEMENT LEAD
description:
##### **ABOUT PERATON**


Peraton is a next\-generation national security company that drives missions of consequence spanning the globe and extending to the farthest reaches of the galaxy. As the world’s leading mission capability integrator and transformative enterprise IT provider, we deliver trusted, highly differentiated solutions and technologies to protect our nation and allies. Peraton operates at the critical nexus between traditional and nontraditional threats across all domains: land, sea, space, air, and cyberspace. The company serves as a valued partner to essential government agencies and supports every branch of the U.S. armed forces. Each day, our employees solve the most daunting challenges that our customers face. Visit peraton.com to learn how we’re keeping people around the world safe and secure.

##### **ABOUT THE ROLE**

**Technology Integration \& Enablement Lead**  

Within Peraton's Space and Mission Solutions sector, there is an immediate need for a cleared Technology Integration \& Enablement Lead to serve as the operational backbone of our emergent technology initiatives. This is a full\-time position based in Reston, VA with flexibility for hybrid work and travel to customer and corporate sites as needed.
